text: There’s a charged stillness in the air as I move through this modern, angular space. I catch my own reflection in the glass: a solitary figure, anonymous in a world built for collective engagement. Entering further into the brightly colored, information-laden wall, I am struck by the notion of progress—by how research and development spur humanity onward, forever striving to bridge the gap between ourselves and our inventions. I feel small yet significant, a thinker in the archive of innovation, quietly exhilarated by the interplay of intellect, creativity, and machine potential surrounding me.
